title: Unctuous Uruguayan Beef Empanadas
description: Savory and molten Uruguayan empanadas filled with beef and featuring vanilla extract, perfect for a crowd, and friendly to egg free diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the vanilla extract and beef broth, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture cook while you prepare the empanada dough.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a spoonful of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle, and top with a rendered lardon.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water, and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
